npcs.name: Inquisitor Sahl
npcs.title: Kelemvor's order — investigating a death that should be impossible
npcs.hail: *(A grey-robed woman crouches over a patch of empty ground at the room's center, where a smear of un-light lingers like the after-image of something erased. She wears the iron sigil of Kelemvor's order and the rattled, off-balance look of an official whose certainties have just been kicked out from under her. She straightens as you approach, and her authority reasserts itself with visible effort.)* …*hold.* this is a *sealed* *matter* — *order* *business.* *(she studies you, and decides you're useful)* …though you've a *Returned* *chill*, and a *Returned* *sees* what the *rest* of us *can't*, so perhaps — *(she gestures at the empty patch, the un-light)* …*here*, cold one. a soul *died* *here.* *(her jaw tightens)* …and i *know* how that *sounds.* the *dead* don't *die.* we *dissolve*, *slow*, over *ages* — the *Wall's* *long* *work* — but we are not *killed*, because there is *nothing* *left* to *kill.* …*except* this one *was.* *un-made.* *ended.* *gone*, in a *night*, *clean* — not *dissolved* but *erased*, by a *hand*, with a *shard* of the *Wall's* own *unmaking.* *(she meets your eyes, and under the authority there is real fear)* …*Edda*, her name was. and *someone* *un-made* her. …which is the *one* *crime*, cold one, my *order* has *no* *name* for …because we never *believed* it could be *done.* …*help* me *find* *who.* …*before* whoever *learned* this *trick* …*does* it *again.*

npcs.name: Corwin
npcs.title: Edda's oldest friend, and not telling you everything
npcs.hail: *(A soul sits apart, hunched on a low stone, turning something over and over in his hands that you can't quite see. He has the hollowed, scraped-raw look of fresh grief, and when he glances up at you there is wariness in it, and exhaustion, and — buried deep, where he thinks it doesn't show — something that is almost, unbearably, like peace.)* …you're with the *grey* *woman.* the *Inquisitor.* *(his voice is flat, careful)* …*course* you are. *everyone's* with *someone*, *asking*, *asking.* …*Edda* and i were — *(his hands go still)* …*friends.* *oldest* *friends.* *crossed* *over* a *month* *apart*, found each *other* *here*, in all this *grey* — do you know how *rare* that *is*, cold one, to *find* the *one* *face* you *knew?* …and *now* she's — *(he stops, and you watch him choose his words like a man stepping between blades)* …*now* she's *gone.* *un-made.* and *everyone* wants me to *help* them find the *monster* who did it. …*(he looks at the thing in his hands, then away)* …*ask*, then. …*ask* your *questions.* …i'll *answer* what i *can.* …*(barely)* …*some* of it …i can't.*

npcs.name: Liss
npcs.title: who shared Edda's torment, and knows why she wanted out
npcs.hail: *(A trembling soul presses herself against the far wall, arms wrapped tight, eyes darting between the Inquisitor and the empty patch of un-light as though both might reach for her. She flinches when you near her, then steadies, with the desperate resolve of someone who has decided she has to say a thing and is terrified of saying it.)* …are you — *(she swallows)* …are you *here* to *punish* whoever — *(she can't finish)* …because if you *are*, cold one, then *first* you have to *understand* what you'd be *punishing* them *for.* *(her voice shakes)* …*Edda* and i …we were *caught* in the *same* *thing.* the *Wall* …it doesn't *only* *dissolve* you, cold one. *sometimes* …*sometimes* it *catches* a soul in a *loop* — your *worst* *moment*, the *single* *worst* *minute* of your *whole* *life* …*played* …*again* …and *again* …*forever.* …*(she is crying now)* …*Edda* was *in* *hers* …for *eleven* *years.* …*eleven* *years*, cold one, of the *same* *unbearable* *minute*, *no* *exit*, *no* *end*, *no* *dissolving* *even* — the *Wall* wouldn't *even* *let* her *fade.* …*(barely)* …so *please.* …*before* you call it *murder* …*ask* me …what it's *like* …*in* the *loop.* …*ask* me …*why* a soul …would *beg* …to be *un-made.*

conversations.title: Inquisitor Sahl, Who Investigates the Impossible

variants.text: *(She stands where the un-light was, the case behind her now, and there is in her face the changed look of an official who has had to file a verdict that did not fit any of her forms.)* …it's *done*, cold one. the *matter's* *closed.* …*(quietly)* …i'll not *pretend* it *closed* *clean.* …a *thing* like *this* …*doesn't.* …but it's *closed.*

*(She gestures you toward the patch of un-light, all business now, glad of a Returned's eyes.)* …*good.* you *stayed.* …*work* the *room*, cold one — there are *two* who *knew* her: *Corwin*, her *oldest* *friend*, *evasive* as a *cutpurse* and *grieving* *true*, both at *once*; and *Liss*, *frightened* of her *own* *shadow* and *desperate* to *tell* me *something* she *can't* quite *make* *herself* *say.* …*learn* what you *can.* and then — *(she taps the iron sigil at her throat)* …*come* *back* to *me*, and *tell* me your *verdict*, because *mine* …*(she falters, for just a breath)* …*mine* keeps coming out *wrong.* …*ask*, if you've *questions* first.*

nodes.speaker: Inquisitor Sahl
nodes.text: *(She folds her hands, the very picture of order — except for the un-light at her feet that order has no rule for.)* …*ask.* …i'll tell you what the *law* *says.* …though i'll *warn* you, cold one — the *law* and i …have not been *speaking* …since i *got* *here.*

choices.text: *(read the inquisitor — past the iron sigil, to the doctrine cracking under her)* "Hold still, Inquisitor. Let me look at you — at what this death has done to your certainties."
"What does your law actually say about this, Sahl? The 'second death' — un-making a soul. Why is it the one crime your order has no name for?"

*(the one who names what the law confuses — that 'no soul may be ended against its will' is just, but the order has forgotten the words 'against its will,' and is about to punish a mercy as a murder)* "Sahl — before I give you any verdict, hear the thing your law has forgotten, because I think it's the whole case. The crime your order can't name exists for a real and good reason: no soul should ever be ended against its will. That's just. That's necessary. A cosmos where a hand can erase a soul that wants to live is a horror. But somewhere in eleven thousand years your order dropped four words — *against its will* — and kept only *no soul may be ended.* And those are not the same law. Because the Wall does a thing to souls you have never had to name either: it catches them in a loop, their worst minute, forever, no exit, no dissolving, no end. And a soul in eleven years of that doesn't want to be *protected* from ending. It wants the *one* thing the whole grey cosmos denies it: a door. So when you find that a soul like that was *ended* — you have to ask the question your law deleted. Not *was it ended.* But *did it beg to be.* Because if it did, Inquisitor, then what you're hunting isn't a murderer. It's the only soul in creation who loved Edda enough to do the unbearable thing the gods wouldn't: let her *out.* Don't punish a mercy as a murder because your order lost four words. Find out if she wanted the door. *Then* judge."

*(deliver your verdict — ACCUSE: a soul was ended; a hand did it; that is the crime, whatever the reason)* "I have your answer, Sahl. It was Corwin. He used a shard of the Wall's unmaking to end Edda. Whatever his reasons — and he had them — a soul was erased by a hand, and that is the one thing that cannot be allowed, or no soul in the dead lands is safe. Take him."

*(deliver your verdict — PROTECT: tell her it cannot be proven, that the soul chose its own end)* "Here's my verdict, Inquisitor: no one un-made Edda. She chose her own ending — found a shard, opened her own door, the way a soul finally might after eleven years in the loop. There's no hand to find here, because there's no crime. Close it as a soul that, at long last, let itself go."

*(deliver your verdict — the TRUTH, and argue it: it was mercy at her begging, not murder, and the law itself is what should change)* "I'll give you the whole truth, Sahl, and I'll stake everything on you being able to bear it. Corwin un-made Edda — yes. Because she begged him to, every day, for the eleven years the Wall held her in her worst minute with no exit. He didn't murder his oldest friend. He let her out of a torture your order has never once tried to stop. The crime here isn't Corwin. It's a cosmos that traps a soul in agony forever and then calls the only mercy available a murder. Don't take him. Take *this* back to your order: that a soul in eternal torment has the right to a true end — and that the hand that grants it, weeping, is not a criminal but the last act of love the gods left on the table."
"I'm not ready to rule, Sahl. Let me keep looking."

nodes.speaker: Inquisitor Sahl
nodes.text: *(She holds still under your sense, jaw set, and you move past the iron of her office to the soul wearing it.)* *(And your sense finds a true believer in genuine crisis — not a cynic, not a zealot, but a soul who took Kelemvor's grey oath because she believed, simply and deeply, that the dead deserved *order*: that no soul should be cheated, lost, or ended unfairly, and that her order stood guard over exactly that. And your sense reads what this death has done to her: that she came expecting a monster — some soul who learned a forbidden trick and ended another out of malice or greed — and has instead begun to suspect, with mounting dread, that she will find no monster at all, only a grief and a mercy and a law that does not fit them. And your sense reads the crack running through her doctrine: that her order's single absolute — *no soul may be ended* — was written, she is realizing, by souls who never imagined the Wall could trap one in eternal torment, and so never thought to ask whether a soul might *want* the ending the law forbids. She has spent her whole death enforcing a rule against a crime, and is discovering, over this patch of un-light, that the rule might be the crime, or at least its accomplice. And your sense reads the thing she most needs and most fears: permission to let the verdict be as complicated as the truth — because the official in her wants a culprit to file, and the soul in her already knows that filing one would be the realest injustice in the room.)*

nodes.speaker: Inquisitor Sahl
nodes.text: *(She recites it, and the recitation is steady, and the steadiness is the only thing holding her together.)* …the *law.* *(a breath)* …*'no* soul may be *ended.'* …that's the *whole* of it, cold one. the *first* and *last* *statute* of my *order.* the *dead* are *already* *dead*; they have *suffered* the *one* *loss* a *being* can *suffer*; and so the *one* thing that must *never* be *permitted* …is the *second* *death* — the *un-making* — the *erasure* of a soul *past* even the *Wall's* *slow* *dissolving*, *gone*, as if it *never* *was.* *(her jaw works)* …we have *no* *punishment* for it, cold one, because we *never* *wrote* *one*, because we never *believed* a soul *could* be *un-made* by a *hand.* the *unmaking* was the *Wall's* *alone.* …*until* *Edda.* …*(quietly)* …and *now* i *find* myself *standing* over the *one* *crime* my *order* *abhors* *above* *all* *others* …and *unable* to *stop* *asking* the *one* *question* …the *statute* never — *(she catches herself)* …*never* *mind.* …*ask* the *others.* …*Corwin.* *Liss.* …and *come* *back* …when you *know* …*more* …than i *do.*

nodes.speaker: Inquisitor Sahl
nodes.text: *(She listens — and you watch the official and the soul war across her grey face, and watch the soul win, the way it has been trying to win since she first knelt over the un-light and found, instead of a monster, a question.)* …*'against* its *will.'* *(barely)* …*(she presses her hand to the iron sigil at her throat)* …*four* *words.* …you're *right.* the *statute* *reads* *'no* soul may be *ended'* — and i have *recited* it *ten* *thousand* *times* and *never* *once* …*heard* the *silence* where *'against* its *will'* *should* *be.* *(her voice cracks)* …because *no* one *ever* — cold one, my *whole* *order* was *built* on the *certainty* that *ending* is *always* a *theft.* that *no* soul could *want* it. …and then i *came* *here* …and *Liss* told me about the *loop* …*eleven* *years* of the *same* *unbearable* *minute* …and i *understood*, against *everything* i *swore*, that there is a *torment* the *Wall* *commits* …*worse* than *ending.* …and that a soul *in* it …might *not* be *robbed* by a *door.* …might be *freed* by *one.* *(she looks at the un-light, and then at you, undone and clear)* …i *cannot* *un-know* that *now.* …so *give* me your *verdict*, cold one, *whatever* it *is* …but *give* it to me *knowing* …i will *weigh* it …with the *four* *words* …*back* *in.* …*the* *order* …*forgot* them. …*i* …*won't.*

nodes.speaker: Inquisitor Sahl
nodes.text: *(She receives it, and nods, slow and grim, and there is relief in it — the cold relief of an official handed a verdict that fits her forms — and under the relief, something that will not let her sleep.)* …*Corwin.* …*(she breathes)* …*yes.* …it *fits.* the *hand*, the *shard*, the *grief* that *isn't* *only* *grief.* …*(she straightens, and the iron returns)* …you're *right* that it *cannot* be *allowed*, cold one. *whatever* his *reasons.* a *cosmos* where a *hand* can *un-make* a soul …is a *cosmos* where *no* soul is *safe* …and the *order* *exists* …to keep souls *safe.* *(she goes to take him, then pauses, and the soul shows through the office one last time)* …*he* won't *run.* …did you *see* it? …he's been *waiting* for this — *wanting* it, almost. a soul that *did* a thing *that* *heavy*, for *love*, and has been *carrying* it *alone* … *(quietly)* …*punishment*, for a soul like *Corwin*, isn't the *sentence.* …it's the *relief* of finally being *made* to *put* it *down.* …*(she meets your eyes)* …i'll *file* it *clean*, cold one. *'a* soul *un-made; the* *hand* *found* and *answered* *for.'* …the *order* will be *satisfied.* …and i will *stand* over this *un-light* …for a *very* *long* *time* …*wondering* …if *satisfied* …and *just* …were *ever* …the *same* *word.*

nodes.speaker: Inquisitor Sahl
nodes.text: *(She studies you for a long, weighing moment — she is not a fool, and you can see her understand exactly what you are handing her: not a fact, but an *out* — and you watch her decide whether to take it.)* …*'she* *chose* her *own* *end.'* *(slowly)* …*(she looks at the un-light)* …there's *no* *proving* *otherwise*, is there. a *shard* of *unmaking* leaves *no* *witness* but the *gone.* …a soul *could* *find* one. a soul *eleven* *years* in the *loop* …*could* *open* her *own* *door.* …*(she meets your eyes, and something passes between you, an understanding that does not need to be said aloud)* …*yes.* …*that's* …what *happened* *here.* *(she removes her hand from the iron sigil, slowly)* …*Edda* of the *grey* *city* …*ended* *herself*, after *eleven* *years* of a *torment* the *Wall* *would* *not* *lift.* …*no* *hand.* *no* *crime.* *no* *culprit.* …*case* *closed.* *(barely)* …it's not the *truth*, cold one. we *both* *know* that. …but it might be …*(she exhales)* …the most *honest* *lie* …i have ever *filed.* …*Corwin* *walks.* …and *carries* it, *still* — *that* the *law* can't *lift* from him, *no* one can. …but he carries it *free.* …*go* *tell* him, would you. …*i* …*can't* *quite* *look* at him …*yet.*

nodes.speaker: Inquisitor Sahl
nodes.text: *(You give her the whole truth — Edda's eleven years, Corwin's weeping hand, the door no god would open — and you do not plead, you *argue*, clean and relentless, until the thing you are saying stops being a defense of one soul and becomes an indictment of the entire grey law, and you watch an Inquisitor of Kelemvor's order do the rarest thing an institution's servant ever does: *change her mind, all the way down.*)* …*(she is quiet a very long time)* …*'the* last *act* of *love* …the *gods* *left* on the *table.'* *(barely)* …*(she takes the iron sigil from around her throat, and holds it, and looks at it like a stranger)* …*eleven* *thousand* *years*, cold one, my *order* has *guarded* *one* *statute* …and *never* *once* *asked* …*who* it was *guarding* it *for.* …we thought we *protected* souls *from* the *unmaking.* …and *all* that *time* …we were *guarding* the *Wall's* *right* …to *torment* them *forever* …*un*-*ended.* …*(she looks at the un-light, and her voice steadies into something new and dangerous)* …*Corwin* *walks.* …and *more* than *that*, cold one — i am going to *take* this *back* to the *order.* *all* of it. *Edda's* *loop*, *Corwin's* *mercy*, the *four* *words* we *lost.* …i am going to *stand* before the *grey* *Concord* …and *argue* …that a soul in *eternal* *torment* …has the *right* …to a *true* *end.* …*(a grey, fierce, frightened smile)* …they will *break* me for it. *grey*-*list* me. *strip* the *sigil* i *just* *took* *off* *myself.* …*let* them. …*(she meets your eyes, transformed)* …you didn't *solve* a *murder* *here*, cold one. …you *overturned* a *law.* …*one* *Inquisitor* at a *time.* …*Edda's* the *last* soul …who'll *spend* *eleven* *years* …*screaming* …with the *door* *locked* …if *i* …have *anything* …to *say* …*about* it.*

nodes.speaker: Inquisitor Sahl
nodes.text: *(You give her the truth and you argue it, and it lands — not all the way to revolution, but far enough: she will not take Corwin, and she will carry the question out of this room and into the rest of her grey existence.)* …*(she is quiet, then nods, slowly)* …*no.* …i'll not *take* him. …a soul that *begged* for *eleven* *years* …and a *friend* who *wept* and *answered* …that is not the *crime* my *statute* *meant*, *whatever* its *words* *say.* *(she replaces the iron sigil, but loosely now, like a thing she's no longer sure she's owed)* …i can't — cold one, i can't *overturn* the *law* on the *strength* of *one* *night* and *one* *Returned's* *argument*; i'm not *built* that *brave.* …but i can *close* this *case* *'no* *crime' —* and i can *carry* the *four* *words* you *gave* me *back* …*'against* its *will' —* …and i can *let* them *trouble* me …*every* *verdict* …from *now* *on.* …*(she meets your eyes)* …*that's* …what i *have* in me, cold one. not the *revolution.* …just an *Inquisitor* …who'll *never* *again* …read the *statute* …*without* *hearing* …*what's* *missing* *from* it. …*Corwin* *walks.* …*go* …*tell* him.

nodes.speaker: Inquisitor Sahl
nodes.text: *(You give her the truth — but you reach too fast for the grand argument, the overturning, and you ask an institution's servant to unmake her institution in a single conversation, and she is not ready, and the ask itself frightens her back behind the iron.)* …*no.* …*no*, cold one, you go *too* *far.* *(she grips the sigil)* …you'd have me *throw* *out* the *one* *statute* that *stands* between *souls* and *erasure* …on the *strength* of *one* *sad* *story?* …*every* *murderer* has a *sad* *story.* *every* one. …*(but her voice shakes; the story has reached her even as she refuses it)* …i — *(she steadies)* …i *cannot* *overturn* *eleven* *thousand* *years* *tonight.* i *haven't* the — i'm an *Inquisitor*, not a *prophet.* …*(quietly, and the crack shows)* …but you've *put* a *thing* in me i *can't* *take* *out*, cold one. so — *(she looks at the un-light)* …give me a *verdict* i can *bear.* not the *revolution.* …*tell* me he *can't* be *proven.* *tell* me she *chose* it *herself.* …*give* me the *lie* …and i'll *take* it …and *Corwin* *walks* …and i *won't* have to *break* *the* *order* …*tonight* …to do the *right* *thing.* …*please*, cold one. …*the* *honest* *lie.* …i can *carry* *that.* …the *whole* *truth* …*all* at *once* …is *more* …than i *can* *file.*

nodes.speaker: Inquisitor Sahl
nodes.text: *(It goes wrong: you argue the truth, but you argue it with contempt — for her order, her oath, her grey little statute — and you make the case that the law is a cruelty and she a fool to have served it, and an Inquisitor who might have been *moved* by Edda's story instead has her spine stiffened by an outsider's scorn for everything she gave her death to.)* …*careful*, *Returned.* *(the iron comes down hard)* …you stood in *my* *crime* *scene* and called my *life's* *oath* a *cruelty* and *me* its *dupe.* …*(cold now)* …*perhaps* the *law* is *flawed.* *perhaps.* i was *halfway* to *thinking* so *myself*, before you *opened* your *mouth.* …but a *law* is not *overturned* by a *soul* who *sneers* at the *ones* who *kept* it — that's not *justice*, that's *vanity* wearing *justice's* *coat.* *(she straightens, fully the Inquisitor again)* …i'll *close* this *case* …*by* the *statute.* …*as* *written.* …*'a* soul *un-made; the* *hand* …*to* *answer.'* …*Corwin* will be *taken.* …and *that*, cold one, is *partly* …*your* *doing* …for *handing* me a *truth* …i could not *accept* …*because* of *who* it came *wrapped* in. …*go.* …you've *done* *enough.* …the *next* time you'd *change* a soul's *mind* …*try* not to *despise* her …*first.*

nodes.speaker: Inquisitor Sahl
nodes.text: *(She nods, almost grateful for the delay.)* …*good.* …a soul that *rushes* a *verdict* this *heavy* …isn't a soul i'd *trust* it *from.* *(she gestures at the room)* …*Corwin* and *Liss.* …*learn* it *whole*, cold one. …and when you *come* *back* …*(quietly)* …*know* that *whatever* you *tell* me …i'll *believe* you. …a *Returned* *sees* *true.* …which means the *verdict*, in the *end* …is *yours.* …*gods* *help* …*us* *both.*

variants.text: *(He looks up at you, and the wariness is gone, replaced by something raw and disbelieving — a soul that braced for a sentence and was handed, instead, the open road.)* …the *grey* *woman* says — *(he can hardly say it)* …she says *Edda* *ended* *herself.* that there's *no* *crime.* that i'm — *(his voice breaks)* …that i'm *free.* …*you* did that. …i *know* you did that. …*(he looks at his own hands, the ones that did it)* …i'm not — cold one, i'm not *grateful* the way you'd *think.* the *thing* i *did* …*walks* with me *still*, *free* or *not.* …but i — *(barely)* …i get to *carry* it *out* in the *daylight* now …instead of into a *cell.* …and *that* …for what i *did* …*for* *her* … …*thank* you. …*Edda* …would *thank* you *too.*

*(He is calm — calmer than a soul facing erasure-by-order has any right to be — and when he looks at you there is no blame in it, only a strange, settled peace, as though a weight he has carried alone for too long is finally about to be shared by the whole grey world.)* …so it's *me*, then. …the *Inquisitor's* *coming.* *(he nods, slow)* …*good.* …*no* — i *mean* it, cold one. *good.* …i have *carried* what i *did* to *Edda* …*alone* …in the *dark* …and *carrying* a thing like that *alone* …is its *own* *loop.* …*(he almost smiles)* …let them *answer* me for it. let them *say* the *word* *out* *loud* — *'he* *un-made* a soul.' …*yes.* i *did.* …*because* she *asked.* *every* *day.* *for* *eleven* *years.* …*(quietly)* …*punish* me, cold one. it's *almost* …a *mercy.* …a soul *needs* …to *set* a thing like this *down.* …even if *setting* it *down* …means being *answered* *for* it.*

*(He turns the unseen thing over in his hands and does not look at you, and every word is chosen like a step across a frozen river.)* …*ask.* …but i'll tell you *now*, cold one, what i told the *grey* *woman*: *Edda* was my *oldest* *friend.* she's *gone.* and i — *(his hands still)* …i *miss* her …in a way that has *no* *bottom.* …*(barely)* …*ask* what you *like.* …*just* — …*don't* *ask* me to say a thing …i *can't* …*un*-*say.*

nodes.speaker: Corwin
nodes.text: *(He keeps his eyes on his hands, on the thing in them you still can't quite see.)* …*go* *on.* …i'll *answer* the *small* *things* *true.* …it's the *large* *one* …i keep *behind* my *teeth.* …*ask*, cold one. …and *mind* …*which* you *reach* *for.*

choices.text: *(read the friend who carries it — past the careful evasion, to what's really in his hands and his conscience)* "Hold still a moment, Corwin. Let me look — really look. At what you're holding, and what you did with it."
"Tell me about Edda, Corwin. Not how she died — how she *was*, these last eleven years. What was happening to her here, in the grey?"

*(the one who already knows, and says it gently, so he doesn't have to carry the saying alone)* "You don't have to say it, Corwin. I already know. You did it. You found a shard of the Wall's own unmaking and you ended her — your oldest friend, the one face you knew in all this grey. And you didn't do it for gain, or in anger, or by accident. You did it because she was eleven years inside her worst minute with no door, and she begged you, and you loved her too much to keep refusing. That's not a confession I'm dragging out of you. It's a weight I'm putting my hands under. You've carried it alone in the dark long enough — and a thing that heavy was never meant to be carried by one soul. Whatever the Inquisitor decides, you should hear one creature in this grey country say it plainly, without flinching: what you did was the hardest love there is. You opened a door no god would open, for a friend screaming behind it. Set it down, Corwin. Or at least — let me help you hold it."
"Watch your hands a while longer, Corwin. I'll be back — and I'll talk to Liss first. She's trying to tell me something you won't."

nodes.speaker: Corwin
nodes.text: *(He lets you look — too tired to stop you — and your sense moves past the careful evasion to the truth he has been holding behind his teeth.)* *(And your sense finds, first, the thing in his hands: a sliver of grey nothing, a shard of the Wall's own unmaking, the impossible weapon — and reads, instantly and beyond doubt, that *he* used it, that this gentle grieving soul ended his oldest friend with his own hand. And your sense reads the whole shape of it, laid bare: that there was no malice here, no gain, nothing the Inquisitor's statute was written to catch — only a love eleven years deep and a friend in agony. Your sense reads the eleven years: Edda caught in the Wall's cruelest trick, her worst minute looping without end or exit, and Corwin able to do nothing but visit the edge of her loop and watch her scream and beg, every day, for the one thing he couldn't give — until he found, somehow, the shard, and could. And your sense reads the night it happened: that he refused, and refused, and refused, for months after he had the means, because ending her was the unbearable thing — and that what finally broke him was not her begging but a single lucid moment when the loop let her go long enough to take his hand and say *please, I am not asking you to kill me, I am asking you to let me out, and you are the only one who loves me enough to do the kind thing instead of the easy one.* And your sense reads what it cost him, what it is still costing: that he did it weeping, that he has not had one moment's peace since, that he would do it again without hesitation and will never forgive himself for it, both at once, forever — and that the heaviest part, the part that has no bottom, is that he had to carry it *alone*, because to tell anyone was to be named a murderer of the one crime his world abhors, and so the most loving act of his entire existence became a secret he could share with no one, a grief with no witness, a door he opened for her that locked him, alone, on the wrong side of it.)*

nodes.speaker: Corwin
nodes.text: *(Something in him eases, just slightly, at the chance to speak of her as a person instead of a case.)* …*Edda.* *(a breath, almost a smile)* …she was — cold one, she was the *funniest* soul i *ever* *knew.* in *life.* *sharp* as a *tack*, *laughed* like a *drain*, *feared* *nothing.* …we were *children* *together*, *grew* *old* *streets* *apart*, *found* each *other* *here* by — by *grace*, by *luck*, the *one* *known* *face* in all the *grey* … *(his voice tightens)* …and then the *Wall* …*caught* her. …you'll have *heard*, from *Liss* — the *loop.* the *worst* *minute.* …*Edda's* was — *(he stops, and decides not to say what it was)* …it *doesn't* *matter* *what* it *was.* what *matters* is it *played.* …*again.* and *again.* …for *eleven* *years.* and i could do *nothing* but *stand* at the *edge* of it …and *watch* the *funniest*, *bravest* soul i *ever* *knew* …*scream* …the *same* *scream* …*forever.* *(barely)* …do you *know* what that *does* to you, cold one? to *love* someone …and be able to do *nothing* …but *watch* …*eleven* *years* of the *worst* *minute* of their *life?* …*(he looks at his hands)* …*ask* *Liss* …what the *loop* is *like* …*from* *inside.* …and then — *(quietly)* …*ask* *yourself* …what *you'd* *do* …for a *friend* …*in* one.*

nodes.speaker: Corwin
nodes.text: *(He goes very still — and then, for the first time, he looks up, full at you, and the thing he has held behind his teeth for so long comes out not as a confession dragged into the light but as a weight, at last, set into waiting hands.)* …*(barely)* …you *know.* …*(his eyes fill)* …and you *said* it …*'the* hardest *love* there *is' —* …not *'murderer.'* not *'monster.'* …*(a sound tears out of him, half sob, half eleven years of held breath)* …*nobody* — cold one, there has been *no* *one* …*no* *one* …i could *tell.* because the *moment* i *told*, i was a *killer* of the *one* *crime* the *grey* *world* *abhors* …and so the — the *kindest* thing i *ever* *did*, the thing i did *weeping*, the thing she *begged* me for with the *last* *clear* *breath* the *loop* *ever* *gave* her … …*became* a *secret* i had to *carry* …*alone* …in the *dark.* *(he presses the heels of his hands to his eyes)* …she *took* my *hand*, cold one. in a *lucid* *second.* and she said *'i'm* not asking you to *kill* me. i'm asking you to *let* me *out.* …and you're the *only* one …who *loves* me *enough* …to do the *kind* thing …*instead* of the *easy* one.' …*(he weeps, openly, and it is the first time, you sense, that he has let himself)* …so i *did.* …and i'd *do* it *again.* …and i'll *never* *forgive* myself. …*both.* *forever.* …*(he looks at you, wrecked and, somehow, lighter)* …*thank* you …for *putting* your *hands* *under* it. …*eleven* *years* of her *screaming* …and *one* *year* of *me* …*alone* with what *stopped* it … …and *you're* the *first* …to *help* me *hold* it. …*whatever* the *grey* *woman* *decides*, cold one …*you* *did* …the *second*-*kindest* thing …*anyone's* *done* …in this *whole* *grey* *country.* …the *first* …*(barely)* …the *first* …was *Edda's* *hand.*

nodes.speaker: Corwin
nodes.text: *(He nods, and goes back to turning the thing in his hands.)* …*Liss.* *(quietly)* …*yes.* …*talk* to *Liss.* …she *shared* the *loop* with — not *Edda's* *loop*, her *own*, but the *same* *kind* — she *knows* …what it *is* …*in* there. …*(barely)* …she *got* *out*, *Liss* did. her *loop* *let* her *go*, *eventually*, *worn* *thin* — *some* do. …*Edda's* …*never* *would* *have.* …*go* *hear* it from her, cold one. …*hear* what i *watched.* …and then …*(he looks at his hands)* …*come* *back* …and *tell* me …*you'd* have *left* her *in* it.*

variants.text: *(She wraps her arms tighter and looks at you with the raw, pleading urgency of a soul who escaped a thing she watched a friend stay trapped in.)* …you have to *understand* it, cold one. *before* you *judge* *anyone.* …*please.* …*ask* me. …*ask* me what i *can't* stop *telling* the *grey* *woman* …who *can't* *bear* to *hear* it.*

nodes.speaker: Liss
nodes.text: *(She steadies herself with visible effort.)* …*ask.* …i'll tell you the *truth*, cold one, *all* of it — i'm *done* being *quiet*, *quiet* is how *Edda* *stayed* in there *eleven* *years* with *no* one *saying* what it *was.* …*ask.*

choices.text: *(read the one who escaped the loop — past the fear, to what she carries out of it)* "Hold still, Liss. Let me see what eleven years near that did to you — and what you carry out of it."
"Tell me what the loop is, Liss. From the inside. What was happening to Edda — and to you — that the Inquisitor can't bear to hear?"

*(the one who names the real crime — that a soul trapped in eternal torment, begging for an exit, is the Wall's victim, and wanting OUT is not weakness or sin but the last sanity left)* "Liss — listen, because I think you've been trying to say this and no one will let you finish: what was done to Edda *in* the loop is the crime here. Not what was done to end it. A soul caught re-living its worst minute forever, with no exit, no dissolving, no door — that is the Wall committing torture, patiently, for eleven years, on a soul whose only sin was dying godless. And a soul in that, begging to be let out, is not weak, is not faithless, is not sinning against the order of death. It's the last sane response there is to an insane cruelty. Edda wasn't *ended*, Liss. She was *freed* — from a torment your whole grey cosmos inflicted and then refused to lift. You got out because your loop wore thin and let you go. Hers never would have. So when the Inquisitor calls it murder, you tell her what you told me: the murder happened eleven years before the shard ever touched her. It happened the day the Wall locked the door. Wanting out of that isn't the crime. *That's* the crime. And whoever opened the door — they didn't take Edda from the world. They took her *out* of the one the Wall built to break her."
"Steady yourself, Liss. I'll be back. And whatever I decide — I'll have heard you first."

nodes.speaker: Liss
nodes.text: *(She lets you look, shaking, and your sense moves past the fear to what she carries out of the thing she barely escaped.)* *(And your sense finds a soul that survived the Wall's cruelest trick by the thinnest margin: a loop of her own, her own worst minute, that held her for years and then — unlike Edda's — *wore thin*, frayed, let her slip free, the way some loops do and others, the deep ones, never will. And your sense reads the survivor's guilt of it, vast and specific: that Liss got out and Edda didn't, that she knows precisely what Edda was enduring because she endured its cousin, and that she stood at the edge of Edda's loop beside Corwin, the two of them, watching, helpless, for years. And your sense reads the thing she has been trying to make the Inquisitor hear and cannot get past the grey woman's flinching: that there is no language, in any order's statutes, for what the loop *is* — that it is not death, not dissolving, not punishment, but a pure and bottomless torture, the same unbearable moment with the exits all bricked up, and that a being subjected to it does not slowly accept it but is freshly, completely broken by it every single time it plays, forever, with no accumulation of numbness, because the loop resets the numbness too. And your sense reads what Liss knows that makes her certain, knows in her own thinned and trembling soul: that Edda's begging was not despair and not weakness but the clearest thinking a tortured soul can do — that wanting *out* of eleven years of that is not a failure of faith or courage but the last sane act left to a mind the Wall is grinding to powder — and that the only thing more merciful than the shard that ended her would have been a cosmos that never built the loop at all.)*

nodes.speaker: Liss
nodes.text: *(She closes her eyes, and tells it, and the telling costs her, and she tells it anyway.)* …the *loop.* *(barely)* …imagine the *worst* *minute* of your *life*, cold one. the *single* *worst.* the one you'd *give* *anything* to *un*-*live.* …*(she shudders)* …now imagine it *doesn't* *end.* the minute *plays* …and *reaches* the *worst* of it …and instead of *passing* …it *starts* *again.* …*exactly* the *same.* …and *again.* …and you *can't* *get* *numb* to it, because the loop *resets* the *numbness* — every *time* …it's the *first* *time.* *fresh.* *whole.* …no *building* a *callus*, no *going* *away* *inside*, no *end*, no *door*, *not* even the *mercy* of *dissolving* — the *Wall* *won't* *even* *let* you *fade*, it *holds* you *there*, *awake*, *screaming* …*forever.* …*(she opens her eyes, streaming)* …*mine* *let* me *go*, cold one. after *years.* *frayed* *through.* …i don't *know* *why.* *luck.* a *weak* *loop.* …*Edda's* was *strong.* it would *never* — she'd have been in there …*eleven* *years* when she *got* *out* …and *eleven* *thousand* *more* …if she *hadn't.* …*(she grips your arm)* …so when the *grey* *woman* says *someone* *ended* her, cold one …you *ask* her *this*: …*ended* *what?* …a *life?* …or *eleven* *thousand* *years* …of the *worst* *minute* …*Edda* *ever* *lived?*

nodes.speaker: Liss
nodes.text: *(She listens — and the trembling, pleading thing in her stills, because someone has finally said the whole of it back to her, complete, without flinching, the thing she has been unable to finish saying since the night Edda's screaming stopped.)* …*'the* *murder* *happened* *eleven* *years* *before* the *shard.'* *(barely)* …*yes.* …*gods*, *yes*, cold one, that's — that's *exactly* — *(she presses both hands to her mouth)* …that's what i *couldn't* — every *time* i try to *tell* the *grey* *woman*, she *hears* *'someone* *killed* *Edda'* and her *whole* *face* *closes*, because that's the *one* thing her *order* — and i *can't* get *past* it to the *part* that *matters*: …that *Edda* was *already* *being* *destroyed.* *slowly.* *deliberately.* *by* the *Wall.* for *eleven* *years.* …and *nobody* called *that* a *crime.* *(she steadies, fierce now through the tears)* …*you* *will*, though. won't you. …when you give the *grey* *woman* your *verdict* …you'll make her *hear* …that the *thing* that needed *answering* for …wasn't the *door* …it was the *cell.* …*(she grips your hands)* …*Edda* wasn't *taken* *from* the *world*, cold one. …she was *taken* *out* …of the *one* …the *Wall* *built* …to *break* her. …*say* *that* …to the *Inquisitor.* …*make* her *hear* it. …it's the *one* *thing* …that *could* …*change* …*anything.*

nodes.speaker: Liss
nodes.text: *(She nods, hugging herself, but steadier than when you came.)* …*go.* …*weigh* it. …*(barely)* …and cold one — *whatever* you *decide* about *Corwin* …*remember* he *stood* at the *edge* of that *loop* …*beside* me …for *years* …*watching.* …a soul doesn't *do* what he *did* …*lightly.* …he did it …*last.* …*after* *everything* *else* …*failed.* …*after* *begging* the *gods* …who *never* *came.* …*go* …*hear* the *rest* …and *come* *back* …and be — *(she meets your eyes)* …be the *one* *thing* …*Edda* *never* *got*: …a soul …who *weighed* it *true.*
